summary: Enthusiastic fans rave about a sweet “Baby Blue” and a soaring Terrapin Station at the RFK show, though no recording‑quality details are provided.
review: Both reviewers give high marks, highlighting Bob Weir’s charismatic band introductions and Jerry Garcia’s fiery Terrapin performance, as well as a memorable Baby Blue. The concert is remembered fondly for its energy and song selection, but the reviews contain no information about the source or technical quality of the recording.

ai_rating.rationale: Strong positive comments about performance (standout songs and band interaction) but only two brief reviews and no mention of recording quality, leading to a modest confidence level.

recording_quality.technical_notes: No reviewers mentioned soundboard, audience mix, or any audio issues; assume acceptable but unverified quality.

show_quality.setlist_flow: Reviewers emphasize the excitement of the late‑night show, noting a strong second‑half momentum with memorable highlights rather than commenting on overall pacing.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Praised for an "awesomely built screaming" Terrapin performance.
band_member_comments.Bob: Noted for his engaging introductions of the band members.
